title: Tanacetum modestum
taxonomy: Tanacetum modestum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Tanacetum, which includes a group of plants commonly known as tansy or feverfew. This genus is part of the tribe Anthemideae, a group of daisy-like plants. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as sunflowers or daisies. Tanacetum modestum is native to the mountainous regions of Europe.
uses: Tanacetum modestum, also known as pyrethrum, has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. intro: Tanacetum modestum, also known as Tanacetum, is a flowering plant in the daisy family, commonly found in Eastern Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, making it beneficial for relieving headaches, fever, and digestive issues.
